title: The intertwined stories of Putin and Stalin's refugees in a literary circle near Paris
subhead: In the Paris suburb of Vanves, a small literary circle keeps alive the memory of the great Russian poet Marina Tsvetaeva, in the apartment where she lived before her doomed return to Stalin's Russia. Today's Russian dissidents see in her verses a reflection of their own inner turmoil.

title: Gulf monarchies divided amid threats of new US strikes on Iran
Trump's withdrawal of 5,000 US troops from Germany marks turning point in relations between the two countries
Jean Pisani-Ferry: 'The United States has lost its moral authority for good'
subhead: The United States is turning its back on democracy and freedom – values the nation once embodied – and is now replacing them with the law of the strongest and a culture of deal-making. Donald Trump has caused irreparable harm to the world's leading power, argues the economist in his column.

title: Illegal rave in France draws 20,000 to 'dangerous' military site
subhead: The rave near the town of Bourges in central France kicked off on Friday. As many as 30,000 people from France and neighboring countries are expected to arrive at the peak of the event this weekend, defying warning about unexploded ordnance.

title: How Paris became the capital of master sushi chefs
subhead: In recent years, a small constellation of Michelin-starred 'sushiya' has appeared in the heart of Paris. In these temples of Japanese tradition, renowned sushi chefs have drawn inspiration from French cuisine and local French products to create remarkable and original flavors.

title: 'He grabbed me by the neck': Women cyclists speak out about violence and sexual harassment
subhead: Amid tense encounters between drivers and cyclists in city centers and with accidents on the rise, women regularly report experiencing sexist assaults. This has even caused some to give up cycling altogether.

Wales's pro-independence party hopes to top polls for first time ever

Cuba calls US sanctions 'collective punishment' during May 1 marches
Russia drastically scales back May 9 parade over fears of Ukrainian drone attacks
subhead: For the first time since 2008, the victory parade will feature no military hardware. In recent months, Ukraine has repeatedly carried out drone strikes deep inside Russia.

title: 'Writing helps us organize our ideas. How will we understand the world if we let AI do the writing?'
subhead: Le Monde columnist Philippe Bernard explores the questions raised by the decline of writing and the rise of artificial intelligence for our cognitive abilities.

title: 'In the war between the US and Iran, both sides are convinced they control the clock'
subhead: Tehran believes Trump needs a deal; Washington thinks the Islamic Republic cannot survive without one, explains Alain Frachon in his column for Le Monde.

title: 'Céline Dion will generate the equivalent of one-fifth of the economic impact of the Paris Olympics'
'The benefits of music are immediate, varied and free of side effects'
'We must never forget why the peace agreement signed between Israel and Lebanon in 1983 collapsed less than a year later'
